chapter_context.summary: Narrates Hezekiah's mortal sickness, his weeping prayer, the sign of the sun's shadow moving backward ten degrees, his healing, and his beautiful song of thanksgiving.

outline.text: Hezekiah's Illness, Prayer, and Sign of Sun's Shadow
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hezekiah's Illness, Prayer, and Sign of Sun's Shadow (38:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Hezekiah's Song of Thanksgiving After Deliverance
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hezekiah's Song of Thanksgiving After Deliverance (38:9-20), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

outline.text: Remedy of Figs and Hezekiah's Inquiry
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Remedy of Figs and Hezekiah's Inquiry (38:21-22), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of Isaiah.

key_themes.title: Prophecy of Judgment
key_themes.description: This chapter explores key facets of prophecy of judgment, revealing how God's holy character directs both judgment and redemption.

key_themes.title: The Holy One of Israel
key_themes.description: Isaiah's signature title for God, highlighting His absolute transcendent holiness, moral purity, and unique relationship with His people.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the warnings and promises of Chapter 38, how does God's double character of absolute holiness and deep comfort speak to your life today?
How does this chapter encourage you to trust in God's promises of ultimate renewal and restoration in times of spiritual or physical exile?

cultural_historical.title: The Role of the Prophet in Israel
cultural_historical.text: Prophets in the Hebrew Bible were not just fortune-tellers, but 'covenant enforcement agents'—called to confront sin, demand social justice, and bring hope of future restoration.
